summary.intent: Fix a popup visibility bug in the combobox component where open/close decisions used 'first focusable result' instead of 'any visible result', causing queries with only disabled matches to incorrectly close the popup.
summary.outcome: Successfully fixed the bug by introducing a visible-item predicate for popup open/close decisions while preserving the focusable-item predicate for highlight navigation. All tests pass, CI is green, and the fix was committed and pushed as c6223af8 (plus the follow-up disabled-visible-results commit).

repo: The combobox in ars-components distinguishes between 'focusable' items (used for keyboard highlight/navigation) and 'visible' items (used for popup open/close decisions) — these must remain separate predicates.
Codex automated review is integrated into the PR workflow via @codex mentions; it posts inline review threads and uses emoji reactions (👀) to signal in-progress review.
The repo uses cargo llvm-cov for coverage reporting on the combobox module, run with --lib --no-fail-fast flags.
CI pipeline order: clippy → formatting → snapshots → workspace check → mutual exclusion → unit tests.

code.finding: Popup open/close logic was inspected around this range; the bug was using first_focusable key rather than first_visible key for open state decisions.
DisabledBehavior and disabled_keys handling is in this region; relevant to distinguishing visible vs focusable items.
Test suite for combobox; new failing tests for disabled-only matches were added here before the fix was implemented.

workflow: Write failing tests first to confirm the bug before patching — the disabled-only match tests were added and verified to fail before the fix was applied.
Run cargo fmt before the full verification chain (fmt check, tests, clippy, llvm-cov, diff --check) to avoid wasted CI cycles from formatting-only failures.
After resolving a Codex review thread, immediately post a new @codex review comment to trigger the next review pass without waiting for manual re-review.
The full local verification chain used: cargo fmt && cargo fmt --check && cargo test && cargo clippy && cargo llvm-cov && git diff --check && git status.

friction: cargo fmt --check caught two formatting diffs in the new helper code after the initial patch, requiring an extra fmt + re-check cycle before committing.
Multiple 2-minute sleep polling loops were needed to wait for Codex review and CI results, adding latency to the feedback cycle.
Codex review took several polling cycles before producing findings, requiring repeated status checks.
open_items: The multi-select blur/pending-item adapter behavior (fixed in c6223af8) relies on adapter-level pointer-down marking — this is a subtle contract that could silently break with future adapter implementations.
CI unit tests were still pending at the time Codex findings arrived; final unit test results were not confirmed in the transcript before the second commit.
